2605.06654.title: Optimizer-Model Consistency: Full Finetuning with the Same Optimizer as Pretraining Forgets Less
2605.06654.paper_summary: Optimizers play an important role in both pretraining and finetuning stages when training large language models (LLMs). In this paper, we present an observation that full finetuning with the same optimizer as in pretraining achieves a better learning-forgetting tradeoff, i.e., forgetting less while achieving the same or better performance on the new task, than other optimizers and, possibly surprisingly, LoRA, during the supervised finetuning (SFT) stage. We term this phenomenon optimizer-model consistency. To better understand it, through controlled experiments and theoretical analysis, we show that: 1) optimizers can shape the models by having regularization effects on the activations, leading to different landscapes around the pretrained checkpoints; 2) in response to this regularization effect, the weight update in SFT should follow some specific structures to lower forgetting of the knowledge learned in pretraining, which can be obtained by using the same optimizer. Moreover, we specifically compare Muon and AdamW when they are employed throughout the pretraining and SFT stages and find that Muon performs worse when finetuned for reasoning tasks. With a synthetic language modeling experiment, we demonstrate that this can come from Muon's strong tendency towards rote memorization, which may hurt pattern acquisition with a small amount of data, as for SFT.
2605.06654.paper_summary_zh: 優化器在訓練大型語言模型（LLMs）的預訓練和微調階段中扮演著重要角色。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了一個觀察結果，即在微調階段使用與預訓練相同的優化器進行完整微調，能夠實現更好的學習-遺忘權衡，即在新任務上表現相同或更好時，遺忘較少，這一點優於其他優化器，並且可能令人驚訝的是，在監督微調（SFT）階段，LoRA也是如此。<lb/>我們將這一現象稱為優化器-模型一致性。<lb/>為了更好地理解它，通過控制實驗和理論分析，我們展示了：1）優化器可以通過對激活的正則化效應來塑造模型，導致在預訓練檢查點周圍形成不同的地形；2）為了應對這一正則化效應，SFT中的權重更新應遵循某些特定結構，以降低在預訓練中學習的知識的遺忘，這可以通過使用相同的優化器來實現。此外，我們特別比較了Muon和AdamW在預訓練和SFT階段的使用情況，發現Muon在微調推理任務時表現較差。<lb/>通過一個合成語言建模實驗，我們證明這可能源於Muon強烈的死記硬背傾向，這可能會在數據量較小的情況下損害模式獲取，正如SFT所示。
2605.06654.author: Yuxing Liu et.al.
2605.06654.authors: Yuxing Liu, Jianyu Wang, Tong Zhang
